LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JANET FRANKLIN RN, MICHELLE VASQUEZ RN, AND, CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. COMPLAINT IS TIME-BARRED. PLAINTIFF WAS TOLD ON DECEMBER 20, 2007 THAT SHE "MAY HAVE NERVE OR TENDON DAMAGE." ON JANUARY 3, 2008 DR. KAO UPON EXAMINING PLAINTIFF'S HAND STATED "WHY DID YOU WAIT SO LONG, YOU NEED SURGERY." THESE EVENTS WOULD PUT A REASONABLE PERSON ON INQUIRY AND WOULD AROUSE SUSPICION. SEE KNOWLES VS. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 118 CAL.APP. 4TH 1290. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (MS. ROBERTS TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT JANET FRANKLIN RN, MICHELLE VASQUEZ RN, AND, CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. NO LEAVE TO AMEND. CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) (302/CWW)JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING AMENDED JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF LUO, AMY YUE YI TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT FRANKLIN RN, JANET VASQUEZ RN, MICHELLE CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL ON THE COMPLAINT, SAID DEFENDANT TO HAVE JUDGMENT THEREON FOR $1,175.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $1,175.00, * COSTS AWARDED TO DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST ONLY * SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JODI ADAMS, and, JAMES SHIEH DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Emmanuel Enyinwa, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 956-6100 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Elana M. Gold, Esq. Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard (415) 334-6880 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all causes of action. Plaintiff must allege facts that show the complaint is not time barred as to Dr. Adams and Dr. Shieh. (See Knowles vs. Superior Court 118 Cal.App.4th 1290.) Fraud allegations insufficiently plead as to both defendants. Order signed in open Court. Counsel waive notice. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JODI ADAMS, and, RANDALL LOW MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Emmanuel Enyinwa, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 956-6100 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Elana M. Gold, Esq. Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard (415) 334-6880 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike punitive damages allegations granted without leave to amend unless court order obtained. No facts pled to support oppression, fraud, or malice. No court order allows claim for punitive damages. (CCP section 425.13.) Remainder of motion to strike moot in view of ruling on demurrer. Order signed in open Court. Counsel waive notice. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JANET FRANKLIN RN, MICHELLE VASQUEZ RN, and, CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Emmanuel Enyinwa, Esq. Law Offices of Emmanuel Enyinwa (415) 956-6100 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Robyn Roberts, of Bjork Lawrence (925) 279-1150 Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendants and moving party; Priscilla P. DeGois, of Oium, Reyen & Ryor, (415) 392-8300 Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendant Dr. Low. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Complaint is time-barred. Plaintiff was told on December 20, 2007 that she "may have nerve or tendon damage." On January 3, 2008 Dr. Kao upon examining plaintiff's hand stated "why did you wait so long, you need surgery." These events would put a reasonable person on inquiry and would arouse suspicion. See Knowles vs. Superior Court (2004) 118 Cal.App. 4th 1290. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. (Ms. Roberts to prepare Order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
